features.description: insert_suppression_comment() writes drift:ignore inline comments into source files
features.motivation: Allows users to suppress individual findings directly in source files without editing drift.yaml; supports Python, TypeScript, JavaScript with correct comment prefixes and signal abbreviations

features.description: drift diff --auto compares fresh scan against .drift-cache/last_scan.json snapshot
features.motivation: Enables fast post-fix feedback loop: run drift analyze once to save snapshot, apply fixes, then drift diff --auto to see score delta and resolved/new findings without specifying commit hashes

features.description: drift explain FINGERPRINT --from-file analysis.json resolves findings from cached JSON without re-running scan
features.motivation: Reduces latency when explaining findings from large codebases; agents can pass a saved analyze output to explain without triggering a full scan

features.description: drift analyze --review opens an interactive per-finding review session for TP/FP feedback collection
features.motivation: Enables in-flow calibration feedback without leaving the terminal; collects verdicts into .drift/feedback.jsonl for calibration cycle

features.description: nudge_baseline_ttl_seconds (default 900) in DriftConfig lets users tune how long a baseline snapshot stays valid
features.motivation: Hardcoded 900s was too short for large repos and untunable; config field replaces persisted TTL on warm load so a change takes effect immediately

features.description: drift analyze --no-cache bypasses parse/signal caches for the current run; drift cache clear removes cache entries with optional --parse-only/--signal-only/--dry-run scoping
features.motivation: Allows users to force fresh analysis without deleting cache entries and to manually invalidate caches when fixtures or configs change
